mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which compresses input costs and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions (Rank IC < 0.01 over 24-month windows).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 2.5% annual threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% annual threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payout capacity, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 revisions show no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5) across multiple expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and capacity underutilization, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances following a > 1.0% monthly drop in INDPRO.
